' Abu Daawood related it (4990), and Al-Albaanee ruled that his narration of the hadith is Hasan. The others who related it are Ahmad (19519); AtTirmidhee (2315); Ad-Daarimee (2702); and Al-Baghawee, in SharhusSunnah (4131).

1 Abu Daawood related it (4868), and Al-Albaanee ruled that it is Hasan. Ahmad (14644) and At-Tirmidhee (1959) related it as well.

indicating that he is afraid others will overhear his words and that he is imparting a secret to you. The act of turning to each side, then, is tantamount to his saying: “I am about to entrust you with a secret.”

12) The Eldest Is Given Precedence In Speech

‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Sahl, Huwaisah Ibn Mas’ood, and Muhaisah Ibn Mas’ood - when these three went to the Prophet #@ in regard to a dispute, ‘Abdur-Rahmaan, the youngest among them, began to speak. The Prophet #% said to him words to the effect of,

“Let the oldest one speak [first].“ The Companions 4 honored their elders and showed mercy to the young among them, which is perhaps why Ibn ‘Umar & refrained from coming to the forefront when he was the youngest

in a gathering. This occurred when the Prophet #% said to his Companions 4,

555 ESS “Inform me of a tree, the example of which is the example of the Muslim. It bears its fruits at all times by the permission of its Lord, and its leaves do not wear away.”

Ibn ‘Umar knew the answer to this question, for he later said, “Tt occurred to me that it is the date-tree, but I disliked to speak when Abu Bakr and ‘Umar # were there without any of them speaking (with the answer). The Prophet # [then] said,

‘It is the date-tree.’

1! Related by Bukhaaree (6142), Muslim (1669), At-Tirmidhee (1422), AnNassaaee (4713), Abu Daawood (4520), Ibn Maajah (2677), Ahmad (15664), Maalik (1630), and Ad-Daarimee (2353).

13) Do Not Interrupt Or Cut Off Someone While He Is Speaking

" Related by Bukhaaree (6044), Muslim (2811), Ahmad (4585), At-Tirmidhee (2867), and Ad-Daarimee (282). And the wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith.

“When the trust is lost, await the Hour.’ The Bedouin asked, ‘And how will it be lost?’ He #8 said,

“When the matter (i.e., positions of leadership) is entrusted to those who do not deserve it, then await the Hour.”

‘The portion of the hadith that is relevant to our discussion here is Abu Hurairah’s saying, ‘The Messenger of Allah #8 continued to speak.” He did not stop or allow himself to be interrupted, because the right to speak was with the person in the gathering who was already speaking - in this case, the Prophet #% - and not with the questioner.

In establishing the said ruling, we can also draw upon the saying of Ibn ‘Abbaas to ‘Ikrimah: ‘Speak to the people once a week,

: and if you refuse except to do otherwise, then twice; and if you want to address them even more often, then three times. And do not be the cause of people becoming bored by the Quran (by addressing them too often or for too long). And do not let me find you going to a people, while they are talking, and speaking to them (with a sermon), thus cutting off their talk and causing them to become bored. Instead, listen [to them and wait], and when they command [or ask] you [to address them], then speak to them while they are in a state of desiring to hear you...”"7!

14) Speak Slowly, Deliberately, And Succinctly

When you speak quickly or long-windedly, it is likely that your

listeners will understand a meaning other than the one you

intended for them to understand. The Prophet # spoke , deliberately and succinctly; in describing his way of talking,

‘Aaisha % said, ‘If a counter were to try to enumerate the things

spoken by the Prophet # when he spoke, he would be able to do

8) Related by Bukhaaree (59) and Ahmad (8512). P Related by Bukhaaree (6337)

so (i.e., so deliberately did the Prophet # speak, for the benefit of his listeners; and so careful was he in choosing his words).” In the narration of Ahmad, she 4 said, ‘The Messenger of Allah # would not speak quickly or hastily as you do; he would pause as he spoke, so that one who heard him could memorize what he said.”""]_ An-Nawawee and Ibn Hajr explained these narrations, saying that the Prophet #¢ would not speak long-windedly, nor would he # speak quickly, with one phrase or idea following immediately upon the one before it; thus he avoided confusing his listeners.

“And lower your voice. Verily, the harshest of all voices is the voice (braying) of the ass.’’} (Quran 31:19)

In lowering your voice, you are showing good manners with people and with Allah 4%. Had there been any merit in the loud, harsh, shrill voice, the donkey would not be singled out with such a voice; and the baseness as well as the stupidity of the donkey is well known. To be sure, then, speaking with a loud voice is bad manners; and when one speaks in a loud, harsh voice, he shows that he has no respect for others. Ibn Zaid said, “Had speaking loudly been a noble way of utterance, Allah 3 would not have given that quality of voice to the ass.”

16) Words And Phrases That You Should Avoid :

Some people might say certain words or phrases that the Shariah prohibited; perhaps they don’t know the ruling of saying such — phrases - they represent the majority - or they know the ruling _ but forget. And worst of all are those who say those phrases on | purpose, with full knowledge that they are perpetrating a Haram

' The hadith, with its different wordings, is related by Bukhaaree (3568),

Muslim (2493), Ahmad (25677), At-Tirmidhee (3639), and Abu Daawood

forbidden) deed. We cannot enumerate all of those phrases and iwords in this book, but we will mention at least some of them.

But before we proceed, we need to keep something in mind. ‘Some people say that as long as one’s intention is correct, it does ‘hot really matter what he says, even if he makes serious mistakes egarding religious issues. This is not true; a person should be ‘areful to the utmost that his speech is limited to what is allowed 4n the Shariah, for one might say words that will be the cause of this destruction, though he does not perceive the implications of ‘what he says. Here are some words and phrases that you should ‘avoid in your speech:

a) Avoid using words which say or imply that another person is a disbeliever (Kaafir), an innovator in the religion (Mubadai’), or an evildoer (Faasiq), unless of course you have clear proofs to back up your claim.

The Prophet #¢ said,

“Whenever any man says to his brother, ‘O Kaafir (disbeliever),’ then it (it meaning the reality of being a disbeliever) returns and becomes binding on one of them.’"")

And this wording is from the narration of Abu Daawood:

abi GB SS Vy vas OS SG as by et yt th “Whenever any Muslim man rules that another Muslim man is

a disbeliever, then either that other man is a disbeliever or else he is a disbeliever.””

In this regard, there is a group of people whom Allah 4 has blinded - having blinded their minds and not their eyes - a group that indiscriminately attacks the honor of other Muslims, labeling them as disbelievers, innovators, or evildoers. It is as if,

"I Related by Bukhaaree (6104), Muslim (60), Ahmad (4673), At-Tirmidhee (2637), Abu Daawood (4687), and Maalik (1844). The exact wording used here is taken from Bukhaaree’s narration of the hadith,

in their minds, Allah 3% has made such attacks a form of worship for them. Yet were we to study the ways of our pious predecessors, the likes of the Companions and the Imams that followed their guidance - such as Abu Haneefah, Maalik, AshShaafi’ee, and Ahmad - we would find them to be very careful in this regard, especially when it came to calling another person a disbeliever. Unless clear proofs that allowed no room for doubt proved the disbelief of a particular person, and unless the proof i was established upon him (meaning, the proof was presented to ; him, and he was given a chance to recant his previous view), they : would not say with their tongues anything to indicate the/ disbelief of that person. Abu Bakrah 4 related that the Prophet i x gave the foriowing se sermon on the dey of An-Nahr:
content_id:
Abu Daawood meriwayatkannya (4990), dan Al-Albaanee menilai bahwa riwayatnya adalah Hasan. Yang lain yang meriwayatkannya adalah Ahmad (19519); At-Tirmidhee (2315); Ad-Daarimee (2702); dan Al-Baghawee, dalam Syarh us-Sunnah (4131).

Abu Daawood meriwayatkannya (4868), dan Al-Albaanee menilai bahwa hadith itu adalah Hasan. Ahmad (14644) dan At-Tirmidhee (1959) juga meriwayatkannya.

yang menunjukkan bahwa ia takut orang lain akan mendengar perkataannya dan bahwa ia sedang menyampaikan rahasia kepada Anda. Tindakan menoleh ke kanan dan kiri itu, dengan demikian, sama maknanya dengan ucapannya: "Saya akan mempercayakan sebuah rahasia kepada Anda."

12) Yang Tertua Didahulukan Dalam Berbicara

'Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Sahl, Huwaisah Ibn Mas'ood, dan Muhaisah Ibn Mas'ood — ketika ketiganya menghadap Nabi mengenai suatu perselisihan, 'Abdur-Rahmaan, yang termuda di antara mereka, mulai berbicara. Nabi berkata kepadanya dengan makna,

"Biarkan yang tertua berbicara [terlebih dahulu]." Para Sahabat memuliakan orang-orang yang lebih tua dan menyayangi yang muda di antara mereka, yang mungkin itulah sebabnya Ibn 'Umar menahan diri untuk tidak tampil ke depan ketika ia adalah yang termuda

dalam sebuah majelis. Hal ini terjadi ketika Nabi berkata kepada para Sahabatnya,

"Beritahukan kepadaku tentang sebuah pohon, yang perumpamaannya seperti perumpamaan seorang Muslim. Ia menghasilkan buahnya setiap saat dengan izin Tuhannya, dan daunnya tidak gugur."

Ibn 'Umar mengetahui jawaban pertanyaan ini, karena ia kemudian berkata, "Terlintas dalam benakku bahwa itu adalah pohon kurma, namun aku tidak suka berbicara sementara Abu Bakr dan 'Umar ada di sana tanpa seorang pun dari mereka yang berbicara (dengan jawabannya). Nabi kemudian berkata,

'Itu adalah pohon kurma.'

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6142), Muslim (1669), At-Tirmidhee (1422), An-Nassaaee (4713), Abu Daawood (4520), Ibn Maajah (2677), Ahmad (15664), Maalik (1630), dan Ad-Daarimee (2353).

13) Jangan Menyela Atau Memotong Seseorang Ketika Ia Sedang Berbicara

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6044), Muslim (2811), Ahmad (4585), At-Tirmidhee (2867), dan Ad-Daarimee (282). Dan redaksi yang disebutkan di atas berasal dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.

"Ketika amanah telah hilang, nantikanlah hari Kiamat." Orang Badui bertanya, 'Dan bagaimana ia akan hilang?' Beliau bersabda,

"Ketika suatu perkara (yakni, jabatan kepemimpinan) dipercayakan kepada mereka yang tidak berhak atasnya, maka nantikanlah hari Kiamat."

Bagian dari hadith yang relevan dengan pembahasan kita di sini adalah perkataan Abu Hurairah, 'Rasulullah terus berbicara.' Beliau tidak berhenti atau membiarkan dirinya disela, karena hak berbicara ada pada orang di majelis yang sudah berbicara — dalam hal ini, Nabi — dan bukan pada si penanya.

Dalam menetapkan hukum tersebut, kita juga dapat mengambil dalil dari perkataan Ibn 'Abbaas kepada 'Ikrimah: 'Berbicaralah kepada manusia sekali dalam sepekan,

dan jika engkau enggan kecuali melakukan selain itu, maka dua kali; dan jika engkau ingin berbicara kepada mereka lebih sering lagi, maka tiga kali. Dan janganlah engkau menjadi penyebab manusia menjadi bosan terhadap Al-Quran (dengan berbicara kepada mereka terlalu sering atau terlalu panjang). Dan jangan biarkan aku mendapatimu mendatangi suatu kaum, sementara mereka sedang berbicara, lalu engkau berbicara kepada mereka (dengan ceramah), sehingga memotong pembicaraan mereka dan membuat mereka bosan. Sebaliknya, dengarkanlah [mereka dan tunggu], dan ketika mereka memerintahkan [atau meminta] engkau [untuk berbicara kepada mereka], maka berbicaralah kepada mereka sementara mereka dalam keadaan ingin mendengarmu...'"

14) Berbicaralah Dengan Perlahan, Terukur, Dan Ringkas

Ketika engkau berbicara dengan cepat atau panjang lebar, kemungkinan besar para

pendengarmu akan memahami makna yang berbeda dari yang engkau

maksudkan untuk mereka pahami. Nabi berbicara dengan terukur dan ringkas; dalam menggambarkan cara bicaranya,

'Aaisha berkata, 'Seandainya seorang penghitung mencoba menghitung hal-hal

yang diucapkan Nabi ketika beliau berbicara, niscaya ia akan mampu

melakukannya (yakni, demikian terukurnya Nabi berbicara, demi kepentingan para pendengarnya; dan demikian berhati-hatinya beliau dalam memilih kata-katanya).' Dalam riwayat Ahmad, ia berkata, 'Rasulullah tidak berbicara dengan cepat atau tergesa-gesa seperti yang kalian lakukan; beliau berhenti sejenak dalam pembicaraannya, sehingga orang yang mendengarnya dapat menghafal apa yang beliau katakan.' An-Nawawee dan Ibn Hajr menjelaskan riwayat-riwayat ini dengan mengatakan bahwa Nabi tidak berbicara panjang lebar, dan beliau juga tidak berbicara dengan cepat, dengan satu ungkapan atau gagasan yang langsung mengikuti ungkapan sebelumnya; sehingga beliau menghindari kebingungan pada para pendengarnya.

"Dan lunakkanlah suaramu. Sesungguhnya seburuk-buruk suara adalah suara (keledai yang meringkik).'" (Quran 31:19)

Dengan merendahkan suaramu, engkau menunjukkan adab yang baik kepada manusia dan kepada Allah. Seandainya ada kemuliaan dalam suara yang keras, kasar, dan nyaring, niscaya keledai tidak akan dikhususkan dengan suara semacam itu; dan kehinaan serta kebodohan keledai sudah sangat dikenal. Maka, berbicara dengan suara keras adalah akhlak yang buruk; dan ketika seseorang berbicara dengan suara yang keras dan kasar, ia menunjukkan bahwa ia tidak memiliki rasa hormat kepada orang lain. Ibn Zaid berkata, "Seandainya berbicara dengan keras adalah cara bertutur yang mulia, niscaya Allah tidak akan memberikan kualitas suara itu kepada keledai."

16) Kata Dan Ungkapan Yang Harus Engkau Hindari

Sebagian orang mungkin mengucapkan kata-kata atau ungkapan tertentu yang dilarang oleh syariah; mungkin mereka tidak mengetahui hukum mengucapkan ungkapan-ungkapan tersebut — mereka adalah mayoritas — atau mereka mengetahui hukumnya namun lupa. Dan yang paling buruk adalah mereka yang mengucapkan ungkapan-ungkapan itu dengan sengaja, dengan pengetahuan penuh bahwa mereka sedang melakukan perbuatan yang haram

Hadith tersebut, dengan berbagai redaksinya, diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (3568),

Muslim (2493), Ahmad (25677), At-Tirmidhee (3639), dan Abu Daawood

(terlarang). Kita tidak dapat menyebutkan semua ungkapan dan kata-kata itu dalam buku ini, namun kita akan menyebutkan setidaknya sebagian di antaranya.

Namun sebelum kita melanjutkan, kita perlu mengingat sesuatu. Sebagian orang mengatakan bahwa selama niatnya benar, tidak terlalu masalah apa yang ia ucapkan, meskipun ia membuat kesalahan serius dalam persoalan agama. Ini tidaklah benar; seseorang harus berhati-hati sepenuhnya agar pembicaraannya terbatas pada apa yang diperbolehkan dalam syariah, karena seseorang mungkin mengucapkan kata-kata yang akan menjadi penyebab kehancurannya, sementara ia tidak menyadari implikasi dari apa yang ia ucapkan. Berikut adalah beberapa kata dan ungkapan yang harus engkau hindari dalam pembicaraanmu:

a) Hindari menggunakan kata-kata yang menyatakan atau menyiratkan bahwa orang lain adalah seorang kafir (Kaafir), pelaku bid'ah dalam agama (Mubtadi'), atau orang fasiq (Faasiq), kecuali tentu saja engkau memiliki bukti-bukti yang jelas untuk mendukung klaimmu.

Nabi bersabda,

"Kapan pun seseorang berkata kepada saudaranya, 'Wahai Kaafir (orang kafir),' maka hal itu (yakni, kenyataan menjadi seorang kafir) akan kembali dan menjadi kewajiban bagi salah satu dari keduanya.'"

Dan redaksi ini berasal dari riwayat Abu Daawood:

"Kapan pun seorang Muslim memvonis bahwa seorang Muslim lainnya adalah kafir, maka entah orang lain itu memang kafir, atau justru ia sendiri yang kafir."

Dalam hal ini, ada sekelompok orang yang telah dibutakan oleh Allah — dibutakan akal pikiran mereka, bukan mata mereka — sekelompok orang yang tanpa pandang bulu menyerang kehormatan Muslim lainnya, melabeli mereka sebagai orang kafir, pelaku bid'ah, atau orang fasiq. Seolah-olah,

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (6104), Muslim (60), Ahmad (4673), At-Tirmidhee (2637), Abu Daawood (4687), dan Maalik (1844). Redaksi yang tepat yang digunakan di sini diambil dari riwayat Bukhaaree tentang hadith tersebut.

dalam benak mereka, Allah telah menjadikan serangan-serangan semacam itu sebagai bentuk ibadah bagi mereka. Namun seandainya kita mempelajari jalan para pendahulu kita yang saleh, seperti para Sahabat dan para Imam yang mengikuti petunjuk mereka — seperti Abu Haneefah, Maalik, Ash-Shaafi'ee, dan Ahmad — niscaya kita akan mendapati mereka sangat berhati-hati dalam hal ini, terutama ketika menyangkut penyebutan seseorang sebagai kafir. Kecuali bukti-bukti yang jelas yang tidak memberi ruang bagi keraguan telah membuktikan kekafiran seseorang, dan kecuali bukti itu telah ditegakkan atasnya (artinya, bukti telah disampaikan kepadanya, dan ia diberi kesempatan untuk menarik kembali pandangannya sebelumnya), mereka tidak akan mengucapkan dengan lisan mereka apapun yang menunjukkan kekafiran orang tersebut. Abu Bakrah meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi memberikan khotbah berikut pada hari An-Nahr:
content_ar: ' Abu Daawood related it (4990), and Al-Albaanee ruled that his narration of the الحديث is حسن. The others who related it are أحمد (19519); AtTirmidhee (2315); Ad-Daarimee (2702); and Al-Baghawee, in SharhusSunnah (4131). 1 Abu Daawood related it (4868), and Al-Albaanee ruled that it is حسن. أحمد (14644) and At-Tirmidhee (1959) related it as well. indicating that he is afraid others will overhear his words and that he is imparting a secret to you. The act of turning to each side, then, is tantamount to his saying: “I am about to entrust you with a secret.” 12) The Eldest Is Given Precedence In Speech ‘Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Sahl, Huwaisah Ibn Mas’ood, and Muhaisah Ibn Mas’ood - عندما these three went to النبي ﷺ #@ in regard to a dispute, ‘Abdur-Rahmaan, the youngest among them, began to speak. النبي ﷺ #% said to him words to the effect of, “Let the oldest one speak [أولاً].“ The الصحابة 4 honored their elders and showed الرحمة to the young among them, which is perhaps why Ibn ‘عمر & refrained from coming to the forefront عندما he was the youngest in a gathering. This occurred عندما النبي ﷺ #% said to his الصحابة 4, 555 ESS “Inform me of a tree, the example of which is the example of the مسلم. It bears its fruits at all times by the permission of its Lord, and its leaves do not wear away.” Ibn ‘عمر knew the answer to this question, for he later said, “Tt occurred to me that it is the date-tree, but I disliked to speak عندما أبو بكر and ‘عمر # were there without any of them speaking (with the answer). النبي ﷺ # [then] said, ‘It is the date-tree.’ 1! Related by Bukhaaree (6142), مسلم (1669), At-Tirmidhee (1422), AnNassaaee (4713), Abu Daawood (4520), Ibn Maajah (2677), أحمد (15664), Maalik (1630), and Ad-Daarimee (2353). 13) Do Not Interrupt Or Cut Off Someone بينما He Is Speaking " Related by Bukhaaree (6044), مسلم (2811), أحمد (4585), At-Tirmidhee (2867), and Ad-Daarimee (282). And the wording mentioned above is from Bukhaaree’s narration of the الحديث. “عندما the trust is lost, await the Hour.’ The Bedouin asked, ‘And how will it be lost?’ He #8 said, “عندما the matter (i.e., positions of leadership) is entrusted to those who do not deserve it, then await the Hour.” ‘The portion of the الحديث that is relevant to our discussion here is أبو هريرة’s saying, ‘الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 continued to speak.” He did not stop or allow himself to be interrupted, لأن the الحق to speak was with the person in the gathering who was already speaking - in this case, النبي ﷺ #% - and not with the questioner. In establishing tقال ruling, we can أيضاً draw upon the saying of Ibn ‘Abbaas to ‘Ikrimah: ‘Speak to the people حين a week, : and إذا you refuse except to do otherwise, then twice; and إذا you want to address them even more often, then three times. And do not be the cause of people becoming bored by the القرآن الكريم (by addressing them أيضاً often or for أيضاً long). And do not let me find you going to a people, بينما they are talking, and speaking to them (with a sermon), وهكذا cutting off their talk and causing them to become bored. Instead, listen [to them and wait], and عندما they command [or ask] you [to address them], then speak to them بينما they are in a state of desiring to hear you...”"7! 14) Speak Slowly, Deliberately, And Succinctly عندما you speak quickly or long-windedly, it is likely that your listeners will understand a meaning other than the one you intended for them لفهم. النبي ﷺ # spoke , deliberately and succinctly; in describing his الطريق of talking, ‘Aaisha % said, ‘إذا a counter were to try to enumerate the things spoken by النبي ﷺ # عندما he spoke, he would be able to do 8) Related by Bukhaaree (59) and أحمد (8512). P Related by Bukhaaree (6337) لذا (i.e., لذا deliberately did النبي ﷺ # speak, for the benefit of his listeners; and لذا careful was he in choosing his words).” In the narration of أحمد, she 4 said, ‘الرسول ﷺ of الله # would not speak quickly or hastily as you do; he would pause as he spoke, لذا that one who heard him could memorize what قال.”""]_ An-Nawawee and Ibn Hajr explained these narrations, saying that النبي ﷺ #¢ would not speak long-windedly, nor would he # speak quickly, with one phrase or idea following immediately upon the one قبل it; وهكذا he avoided confusing his listeners. “And lower your voice. Verily, the harshest of all voices is the voice (braying) of the ass.’’} (القرآن الكريم 31:19) In lowering your voice, you are showing الخير الآداب with people and with الله 4%. Had there been any merit in the loud, harsh, shrill voice, the donkey would not be singled out with such a voice; and the baseness as well as the stupidity of the donkey is well known. To be sure, then, speaking with a loud voice is bad الآداب; and عندما one speaks in a loud, harsh voice, he shows that he has no الاحترام for others. Ibn Zaid said, “Had speaking loudly been a noble الطريق of utterance, الله 3 would not have given that quality of voice to the ass.” 16) Words And Phrases That You Should Avoid : Some people might say certain words or phrases that the Shariah prohibited; perhaps they don’t know the ruling of saying such — phrases - they represent the majority - or they know the ruling _ but forget. And worst of all are those who say those phrases on | purpose, with full العلم that they are perpetrating a الحرام ' The الحديث, with its different wordings, is related by Bukhaaree (3568), مسلم (2493), أحمد (25677), At-Tirmidhee (3639), and Abu Daawood forbidden) deed. We cannot enumerate all of those phrases and iwords in this الكتاب, but we will mention at least some of them. But قبل we proceed, we need to keep something in العقل. ‘Some people say that as long as one’s intention is correct, it does ‘hot really matter what he says, even إذا he makes serious mistakes egarding religious issues. This is not true; a person should be ‘areful to the utmost that his speech is limited to what is allowed 4n the Shariah, for one might say words that will be the cause of this destruction, though he does not perceive the implications of ‘what he says. Here are some words and phrases that you should ‘avoid in your speech: a) Avoid using words which say or imply that another person is a disbeliever (Kaafir), an innovator in the religion (Mubadai’), or an evildoer (Faasiq), إلا إذا of course you have clear proofs to back up your claim. النبي ﷺ #¢ said, “كلما any man says to his الأخ, ‘O Kaafir (disbeliever),’ then it (it meaning the reality of being a disbeliever) returns and becomes binding on one of them.’"") And this wording is from the narration of Abu Daawood: abi GB SS Vy vas OS SG as by et yt th “كلما any مسلم man rules that another مسلم man is a disbeliever, then either that other man is a disbeliever or else he is a disbeliever.”” In this regard, there is a group of people whom الله 4 has blinded - having blinded their minds and not their eyes - a group that indiscriminately attacks the الشرف of other المسلمون, labeling them as disbelievers, innovators, or evildoers.
